commands.name: find
commands.description: Search for files in a directory hierarchy

patterns.text: find all files with a specific extension
patterns.explanation: Recursively finds all Python files starting from the current directory

patterns.text: find files modified in the last 24 hours
patterns.explanation: Finds files under /var/log that were modified within the last day

patterns.text: find and delete all temporary files
patterns.explanation: Finds and removes all .tmp files recursively

patterns.text: find files larger than a certain size
patterns.explanation: Finds regular files larger than 100 megabytes

patterns.text: find empty directories
patterns.explanation: Lists all directories that contain no files or subdirectories

patterns.text: find files and execute a command on each one
patterns.explanation: Compresses every .log file found

patterns.text: search only the current directory without subfolders
patterns.explanation: -maxdepth 1 limits recursion to the starting directory

patterns.text: skip heavy directories like node_modules while finding source files
patterns.explanation: -prune stops descending into matched paths; -o chains the real predicate

patterns.text: run a command on many files with one process (faster than one exec per file)
patterns.explanation: The + form batches paths like xargs for efficiency

commands.name: locate
commands.description: Find files by name using a pre-built database

patterns.text: quickly find a file by name anywhere on the system
patterns.explanation: Searches the file database for paths matching 'myconfig.yaml'

patterns.text: find files matching a pattern ignoring case
patterns.explanation: Case-insensitive search for any file with 'readme' in its path

patterns.text: update the file database before searching
patterns.explanation: Refreshes the database then searches for report.pdf

patterns.text: limit the number of results returned
patterns.explanation: Returns at most 10 matching results

patterns.text: count how many files match a pattern
patterns.explanation: Prints the number of matching entries instead of listing them

commands.name: which
commands.description: Locate the executable for a command in your PATH

patterns.text: find out where a command is installed
patterns.explanation: Shows the full path of the python3 executable

patterns.text: check if a command exists on the system
patterns.explanation: Tests whether docker is available in PATH

patterns.text: find all locations of a command in PATH
patterns.explanation: Lists every matching executable found in all PATH directories

patterns.text: verify which version of a tool you are running
patterns.explanation: Shows the path to confirm you are using the intended installation

commands.name: whereis
commands.description: Locate the binary, source, and manual page for a command

patterns.text: find the binary, source, and man page for a command
patterns.explanation: Shows the paths to the gcc binary, source, and man page if available

patterns.text: find only the binary location of a command
patterns.explanation: Restricts output to just the binary path

patterns.text: find only the man page location
patterns.explanation: Shows only where the manual pages for bash are located

patterns.text: find all known paths for a program
patterns.explanation: Lists binary, source, and manual page paths for python

patterns.text: get a quick description of what a command does
patterns.explanation: Shows the brief description from curl's man page

patterns.text: look up several commands at once
patterns.explanation: Shows one-line descriptions for ls, cat, and grep

patterns.text: check what a command does before using it
patterns.explanation: Displays a brief summary of rsync from the manual database

patterns.text: search for a description using a wildcard pattern
patterns.explanation: Shows descriptions for all commands matching the git wildcard

commands.name: apropos
commands.description: Search the manual page names and descriptions for a keyword

patterns.text: find commands related to a topic
patterns.explanation: Lists all man pages whose descriptions mention 'compress'

patterns.text: search for commands that deal with user accounts
patterns.explanation: Finds commands related to user account management

patterns.text: find all commands related to network
patterns.explanation: Lists man pages mentioning 'network' in name or description

patterns.text: search for a command when you forgot its name
patterns.explanation: Helps rediscover commands like du or df by searching descriptions

patterns.text: use a regex to search man page descriptions
patterns.explanation: Searches using a regular expression to find commands starting with 'copy'

commands.name: type
commands.description: Describe how a command name would be interpreted by the shell

patterns.text: check if a command is a builtin, alias, or external program
patterns.explanation: Tells you whether ls is an alias, shell builtin, or file on disk

patterns.text: see all definitions for a command name
patterns.explanation: Lists every interpretation of 'echo' including builtins and binaries

patterns.text: check if a name is a shell function
patterns.explanation: Shows whether my_function is defined as a shell function and its body

patterns.text: get just the type classification of a command
patterns.explanation: Outputs a single word like 'builtin', 'alias', 'function', or 'file'

commands.name: command
commands.description: Run a command bypassing shell functions and aliases

patterns.text: run the real command instead of an alias or function
patterns.explanation: Executes the actual ls binary, ignoring any alias or function named ls

patterns.text: check if a command exists in a script
patterns.explanation: Tests whether git is available without running it

patterns.text: get the path of a command without running it
patterns.explanation: Prints the path or alias definition without executing anything

patterns.text: bypass a function override in a shell script
patterns.explanation: Ensures the builtin or binary echo is used even if a function named echo exists

commands.name: fd
commands.description: A fast and user-friendly alternative to find

patterns.text: find files matching a pattern with a simpler syntax than find
patterns.explanation: Finds all files and directories containing 'config' in their name under src/

patterns.text: find only files with a specific extension
patterns.explanation: Finds all JSON files recursively from the current directory

patterns.text: find hidden files that are normally ignored
patterns.explanation: Includes hidden files in the search to find dotenv files

patterns.text: find files and run a command on each one
patterns.explanation: Finds all Python files and runs the black formatter on each

patterns.text: find files excluding certain directories
patterns.explanation: Searches for files matching 'test' while excluding node_modules and .git

commands.name: rg
commands.description: Ripgrep - recursively search file contents using regex, very fast

patterns.text: search for a string across all files in a project
patterns.explanation: Searches all files under src/ for the string 'TODO', respecting .gitignore

patterns.text: search only in files of a specific type
patterns.explanation: Restricts the search to Python files only

patterns.text: show context lines around each match
patterns.explanation: Shows 3 lines of context before and after each match

patterns.text: search for a pattern and replace it across files
patterns.explanation: Finds files containing 'oldFunc' then uses sed to replace it

patterns.text: count the number of matches per file
patterns.explanation: Shows how many matches each file contains

patterns.text: search for a literal string that contains special regex characters
patterns.explanation: Treats the pattern as a literal string, disabling regex interpretation

commands.name: ag
commands.description: The Silver Searcher - a fast code searching tool similar to ack

patterns.text: search for a pattern in all source files
patterns.explanation: Searches for the regex pattern in all files under src/

patterns.text: search only in specific file types
patterns.explanation: Restricts the search to JavaScript files

patterns.text: search for a whole word match only
patterns.explanation: Matches the word 'class' but not 'subclass' or 'classify'

patterns.text: list just the filenames containing matches
patterns.explanation: Shows only the file paths instead of the full matching lines

patterns.text: search ignoring certain directories
patterns.explanation: Skips the vendor directory while searching

commands.name: fzf
commands.description: A command-line fuzzy finder for interactive filtering

patterns.text: interactively search and select a file to open
patterns.explanation: Opens fzf to fuzzy-find a file then opens the selection in vim

patterns.text: fuzzy search through command history
patterns.explanation: Pipes shell history into fzf for interactive searching

patterns.text: preview files while searching through them
patterns.explanation: Shows a preview of the highlighted file while browsing

patterns.text: fuzzy find and switch to a git branch
patterns.command: git branch | fzf | xargs git checkout
patterns.explanation: Interactively pick a branch from the list and check it out

patterns.text: select multiple items from a list
patterns.explanation: Allows selecting multiple log files with Tab then deletes them

commands.name: xargs
commands.description: Build and execute commands from standard input

patterns.text: delete files from a list
patterns.explanation: Reads filenames from the file and passes them as arguments to rm

patterns.text: run a command on each line of input separately
patterns.explanation: Downloads each URL from the file by replacing {} with each line

patterns.text: run multiple processes in parallel
patterns.explanation: Resizes PNG images using 4 parallel processes

patterns.text: handle filenames with spaces safely
patterns.explanation: Uses null delimiter to safely handle filenames containing spaces

patterns.text: limit how many arguments are passed at once
patterns.explanation: Passes one host at a time to ping instead of all at once

commands.name: parallel
commands.description: Execute jobs in parallel using multiple CPU cores

patterns.text: run a command on multiple files in parallel
patterns.explanation: Compresses all log files in parallel, one per CPU core

patterns.text: process a list of inputs from a file in parallel
patterns.explanation: Downloads each URL from the file using all available CPU cores

patterns.text: control how many jobs run simultaneously
patterns.explanation: Converts JPG to PNG with at most 4 concurrent jobs

patterns.text: run the same command with different arguments in parallel
patterns.explanation: Runs the echo command for each argument simultaneously

patterns.text: pipe input and process each line in parallel
patterns.explanation: Executes process.sh for each line of tasks.txt in parallel

patterns.text: show a progress bar while processing files in parallel
patterns.explanation: Compresses CSV files in parallel with a progress indicator
